candidates.why: Explicit operating D&A line above financial items, but it is a company-level total and not specifically tied to the selected COGS bucket 'Materials and supplies'. Adding it to COGS would risk misclassification/double counting.
Component of the total D&A row, but not labelled as COGS-specific and therefore not appropriate to add to the selected COGS bucket.
Component of the total D&A row, but not labelled as COGS-specific and therefore not appropriate to add to the selected COGS bucket.
Minor balancing line under the D&A section; not a clear COGS-specific D&A row and should not be used over the summary row.

variables.notes: No explicit depreciation/amortisation row is identified as belonging to cost of sales/materials. The visible D&A rows are separate operating totals/components, not COGS-specific.

candidates.why: Explicit separate operating D&A line above Operating Profit and outside the selected XSGA component rows ('Selling, General & Admin', 'Employee benefits', 'Other Operating Expenses'). Per the same-level separate-line rule, it should be added separately. It is also the summary row, so it is preferred over its components.
Component of the summary D&A row. Do not select when the summary 'Depreciation and Amortization' row is available, to avoid double counting.
Component of the summary D&A row. Do not select when the summary 'Depreciation and Amortization' row is available, to avoid double counting.
Balancing component within the D&A section; inferior to the explicit summary row for separate addition purposes.

variables.notes: The income statement presents D&A as a distinct operating line item at the same level as the selected overhead buckets, indicating it is not embedded in the previously selected XSGA component rows. No separate R&D expense row is present, so there is no XRD interaction preventing inclusion.
